description_long: This is a primitive-style axe featuring a large, oval-shaped stone blade securely fastened to a wooden handle using tightly wound natural fiber cordage. The stone blade is smooth and rounded, with a matte gray coloration, and is lashed horizontally to the top of the handle. The handle is cylindrical, slightly curved, and appears to be made from a single piece of wood, showing a natural brown hue and some irregularities in shape. The binding is extensive, covering the upper portion of the handle and the stone, suggesting a hand-crafted, utilitarian construction. There are no visible metal components or modern fasteners, and the overall appearance is rustic and functional. The axe is likely intended for chopping or striking tasks, and its design evokes traditional or indigenous tool-making techniques.
description: A hand-crafted axe with a smooth stone blade lashed to a wooden handle using natural fiber cordage, featuring a rustic and primitive design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the stone blade and the extensive cord binding securing it to the wooden handle.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the rounded end of the stone blade and the tightly wrapped cordage around the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other face of the stone blade and the continuation of the cord binding.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the length of the wooden handle and the back of the stone blade, with the binding visible at the top.
attribute_handle_type: Cylindrical, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural finish and no added grip features; cross-section is roughly round.
attribute_blade_design: Large, oval-shaped stone blade with a smooth, rounded edge; single striking edge, no beard or poll, utilitarian and undecorated.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional; blade is lashed to the handle with extensive natural fiber cordage.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museo Nacional de Historia Natural de Chile
